title: LKE
subtitle: Managed Kubernetes — without the control plane chores

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: LKE is like hiring a building superintendent. You bring the tenants (your pods); Akamai keeps the elevators (control plane), HVAC (etcd), and security (master nodes) running 24/7.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
LKE = Linode Kubernetes Engine. Akamai runs and patches the Kubernetes control plane for free. You only pay for worker nodes (which are Linodes). You get:

- A kubeconfig in one CLI call
- HA control plane available as a paid add-on
- Built-in NodeBalancer integration via Service type=LoadBalancer
- Block storage CSI driver for dynamic PVCs

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Provision: linode-cli lke cluster-create --label prod --k8s_version 1.30 --region us-east --node_pools.type g6-dedicated-4 --node_pools.count 3. Add a GPU pool for inference workloads. Use KEDA for event-driven autoscaling on top of HPA.
"LKE = vanilla Kubernetes you actually own — control plane is free, you only pay for worker Linodes."

quiz.question: Who pays for the LKE control plane?

options.text: You — at the same rate as a Linode
options.explain: Akamai runs the control plane for you at no charge.

options.text: Nobody — the control plane is free
options.explain: You only pay for worker nodes. HA control plane is a separate paid add-on.

options.text: You run the control plane yourself
options.explain: In LKE, Akamai runs the control plane. If you want self-managed, use plain Linodes with kubeadm.

quiz.question: How do you expose a service with a public IP on LKE?

options.text: Manually create a NodeBalancer and patch each pod
options.explain: LKE handles this automatically — no manual patching.

options.text: Set Service type=LoadBalancer; LKE provisions a NodeBalancer
options.explain: LKE's cloud-controller-manager auto-creates a NodeBalancer with a public IP when you set type=LoadBalancer.

options.text: Use a ClusterIP and hope for the best
options.explain: ClusterIP is internal-only — no public exposure.
It's the standard pattern for LKE.
